metadata.title:
PRATT SCORED FOR


HIGH HANDED ACTION

Continued from PAGE One.

city council, Mr.. Robertson said:

pages.raw_text:
city council, Mr.. Robertson said:


AFTER the decision Of the court
one would have thought that Mayor
Pratt would await the decision OF
the city council. Instead Of that he
caused copy of the charges to be
filed with the board Of police com.
mfssioners, which is now attempt.
Ing to try them, without the testi.
mony Of the plaintiff being heard
I have never seen an act SO fla.
grant and in such open violation OF
the law. There is no politics In
this thing / don't care whether
Mayor Pratt is democrat or re.
publican, this matter should be In.
vestigated on its merits and the
truth heard. / appear here for J.
H. Elliott, an honest and just old
man, a defender of the Union, and
a member of the G.. A. R., who has
filed these charges. There are no
gamblers, saloonmen or bad women
behind this case, and when 1t is
heard we want priests and preach.
ers, and all other citizens, to be
present. We want only justice and
the fair name of the city of Spo-
kane cleansed of the stain that has
been applied to it through the &C
tons set forth in these charges. No
subordinate board ought to have
the right to forestall justice in this
case"


The board of police commission.
ers was ordered to make no find
ings in its present inquiry until
after the city council acted, and 1t
was further ordered to see that
John T. Sullivan was on hand when
the official trial is called.


|. At the outset there was talk Of an
early date for trial of the Sullivan
charges, and Councilman Lambert
introduced a resolution setting the
hearing for October 81. On account
of the absence from the city Of the
sewer, water and light committee
about this time 1t was agreed to set
the date for a week later.


WHAT LAMBERT SAID.


In support Of his resolution Coun.
Gilman Lambert said: "The resig.
nation under fire Of Acting Chief OF
Police Sullivan makes no difference
in this case. This matter of the con.
duct of the police has been before
the people for months and we have
been asked to inquire into it. The
court has decided that the right is
ours to hear the case, and l am In
favor of going ahead with the hear.
ing as originally intended."


Councilman Nelson seconded the
Lambert resolution. - 4.


Councilman Mohr wondered what
would happen II the council found
Sullivan guilty, but 1t was agreed
that that wa sa matter Of after con-
sideration.


Councilman Shaefer objected to
the date, as being inconvenient for
the members Of the committee look
ing up the question of power site,
